relations.note: Adjacency enforcement relies on the declared background color in section metadata.
Assembly-time footnote assignment uses the declared consumed footnote symbols.
Reinforces self-contained sections by forbidding cross-boundary decorative dependence.

quotes: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color
no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking
Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color

rules.intent: Guarantee reliable modular stacking across email clients.
rules.rule_text: Each email section is a self-contained <table>-based block at 600px (size.canvas.email_width_600) with its own top/bottom 32px padding (padding.section.top_bottom_32 = 32px) and a declared background color; there are no margins outside the section — assembly is direct stacking.

quotes: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

rules.intent: Provide the assembler the declared facts needed to stack sections and resolve footnotes/backgrounds safely.
rules.rule_text: Section metadata must declare: the background color, whether the section establishes dermatomyositis (DM), which footnote symbols (†, ‡ — assigned at assembly to avoid collisions) it consumes, and whether it contains a CTA.

quotes: Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Avoid footnote-symbol collisions when independently authored sections are stacked.
rules.rule_text: Footnote symbols are superscript and assigned in order of appearance at assembly time; when more than one referenced claim exists, sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., {{fn1}}) rather than hard-coded daggers.

quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

rules.intent: Keep sections self-contained so core-inserted locked components don't break decorative continuity.
rules.rule_text: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections placed adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above or below (e.g., a wave meant to continue into the footer).

effect.reserved_for: non-adjacent placement (not next to another dark section)
effect.subject: dark_background_section

quotes: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections
Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Preserve alternating background rhythm and visual contrast between stacked sections.
rules.rule_text: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.intent: Ensure cross-client email rendering, especially Outlook.
rules.rule_text: All colors in code must be HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only for email-client compatibility; and bgcolor attributes must be duplicated for Outlook.
